question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isPersistBytes() returning false (the default) to cause streamFile.setBytes(null) to be called, then trigger a NullPointerException in downstream code that assumes bytes are always present, causing a denial-of-service crash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isWriteFiles() flag being true to cause Utility.archiveFile() to be called with a stream file path derived from an attacker-controlled filename, writing arbitrary bytes to a predictable filesystem location and potentially overwriting critical files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteSignatures() being true to cause signature bytes from all nodes to be archived, and if signature filenames contain null bytes or special characters, cause the archiving logic to write to unintended filesystem paths?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getFrequency() value being logged in downloadAndParseSigFiles() to infer internal configuration details (e.g., polling interval) from log output, aiding in timing attacks against the download pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the permanent nature of setEnabled(false) (no automatic re-enable mechanism) to cause a one-time denial-of-service that persists across the lifetime of the process without requiring repeated attacker interaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getPathPrefix() field being set to an attacker-controlled value to redirect cloud storage path lookups to a different bucket prefix, causing the importer to download files from an attacker-controlled path within the same bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getPathType() being set to AUTO to cause the importer to probe multiple path formats (ACCOUNT_ID and NODE_ID), increasing the attack surface for path-based injection or enumeration of node identifiers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getSources() list being mutable to inject a malicious StreamSourceProperties entry pointing to an attacker-controlled endpoint, causing the importer to fetch stream files from an untrusted source?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getRegion() field being set to an invalid or attacker-controlled AWS region string to cause the S3 client to connect to an unexpected endpoint, potentially enabling SSRF or credential theft?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isEnabled() is checked only at the start of downloadNextBatch() (not atomically throughout the batch) to inject a large number of malicious signature files that are processed even after the downloader should have been disabled?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit setWriteFiles(true) combined with a crafted stream file whose getName() returns a path with symlink components, causing Utility.archiveFile() to follow the symlink and overwrite sensitive files outside the archive directory?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit setPersistBytes(true) to prevent streamFile.setBytes(null) from being called, causing all downloaded stream file bytes to accumulate in the lastStreamFile AtomicReference cache, leading to unbounded memory growth and eventual OOM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().isAnonymousCredentials() returning true for certain network names to cause the importer to use anonymous cloud access, allowing the attacker to serve files from a public bucket they control under the same bucket name?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getTimeout() being set to an extremely large value to cause download goroutines to hang indefinitely, exhausting the Schedulers.parallel() thread pool and causing a denial-of-service on all concurrent downloads?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getPathRefreshInterval() being set to a very short duration to cause excessive address book refresh operations, amplifying load on the consensus node service and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of isWriteFiles() being true and a stream file with a very long filename to cause a filesystem path length overflow in Utility.archiveFile(), resulting in a FileOperationException that silently skips verification of that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteSignatures() being true to cause the importer to write signature bytes from all nodes to disk, and if the signature bytes contain executable content, potentially trigger code execution if the archive directory is in a monitored or auto-executed path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType returned by getStreamType() being used as a metric tag (e.g., in cloudStorageLatencyMetric) to inject arbitrary strings into the metrics system by influencing the stream type value, causing metric label injection or cardinality explosion in the monitoring backend?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getGcpProjectId() field being set to an attacker-controlled value to cause GCP storage requests to be routed to a different project, potentially accessing files from an unintended GCP project or causing authentication failures?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setEnabled() method being called from within the reactive Flux pipeline (via onErrorContinue) to trigger a state mutation from an error handler, causing the downloader to be disabled as a side effect of a crafted error condition in a stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the listLimit logic in downloadAndParseSigFiles() — where listLimit is set to 1 when isEnabled() is false — to cause only a single signature file to be processed per cycle, effectively throttling the importer to near-zero throughput as a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getAccessKey() and getCommon().getSecretKey() fields being stored in plaintext in the Spring Environment to extract cloud credentials via an exposed /actuator/env or /actuator/configprops endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getBucketName() fallback logic (which derives the bucket name from the network name) to cause the importer to connect to a predictable public bucket by spoofing the network name, downloading attacker-controlled stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit isWriteFiles() being true to cause the importer to write stream files to disk at a rate exceeding the filesystem's write capacity, triggering I/O errors that propagate as FileOperationException and cause the importer to skip valid stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getFrequency() value being used in a log message (toMillis() / 1000f) to cause a division-by-zero or arithmetic exception if the frequency Duration has a zero millisecond value, crashing the logging thread?
